title: An Apology for Lollard Doctrines
summary: The repository XML is a transformed local copy/version of University of Michigan CME item ACM9160.0001.001, an uncorrected OCR/TEI Level 1 encoding of James Henthorn Todd's 1842 Camden Society edition. U-M encoded the title page and medieval text on printed pp. 1–113 while linking all 272 page images and leaving Todd's introduction, notes, glossary, and errata largely as page milestones only. Todd transcribed a single item in Trinity College Dublin MS 245 (legacy C.5.6): corrected fols. 163r–217r, corresponding to old fols. 164–218. Todd explicitly treated Wycliffe's authorship as uncertain; the current TCD item record says this exact item is not by Wycliffe.
review_notes: The medieval work, its sole manuscript witness, Todd's printed edition and apparatus, U-M's OCR and linked scans, independent scans of the edition, and the local XML are separate entities with separate access and rights statements.
Todd's old foliation and Jefferson's corrected foliation are a crosswalk for the same manuscript item, not evidence of two witnesses.
The enclosing TCD catalog title ‘John Wycliffe, tracts’ is legacy collection-level description; the same item record expressly says that the Apology incipit at fols. 164–218 is not by Wycliffe.
Negative public-facsimile findings are date-bounded to 2026-07-11 and do not prove that TCD has never digitized the manuscript or that no private reproduction exists.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for acm9160
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: The root is DLPSTEXTCLASS and the U-M identifier in the header is ACM9160.0001.001.

The checked-in XML is 300,263 bytes. It differs byte-for-byte from the 294,377-byte XML in the archived raw Michigan package: the repository copy adds an XML declaration and NODE identifiers and changes EDITORIALDECL/@N from 1 to 4, among other serialization differences.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item ACM9160.0001.001
entities.description: A publicly accessible uncorrected OCR text with Level 1 TEI-style structural markup and links to page images of Todd's 1842 edition.
notes: The item-specific declaration says the file was created by OCR, was not corrected or content-edited, follows Level 1 of the TEI in Libraries Guidelines, and links digital page images.

The text layer includes Todd's title page and pp. 1–113 of the Middle English work. The introduction (pp. v–lxiii) and back matter (pp. 114–206 plus errata) are represented principally by PB milestones rather than transcribed prose.
The current U-M page calls Wycliffe only ‘supposed author’; that bibliographic field does not settle work-level authorship.

entities.label: U-M 272-TIFF page-image set for ACM9160.0001.001
entities.description: The raw Michigan Book ZIP mirrored by Internet Archive: 272 one-bit TIFF page images of a copy of Todd's 1842 edition, plus U-M XML, plain text, header, pageview data, and ingest logs. It is a facsimile of the printed edition, not of TCD MS 245.

physical_description.extent: 272 TIFF images; 277 files total in a 19,005,255-byte ZIP
physical_description.format: 600 dpi, one-bit TIFF G4 page images plus XML, text, and ingest metadata
notes: The images are named 00000001.tif through 00000272.tif.
pageview.dat maps image 65 to printed page [1] and the sequence through the edition's errata leaf.

entities.reproduction_format: digital page images of a printed edition

entities.label: Todd 1842 edition of An Apology for Lollard Doctrines

bibliographic.citation: An Apology for Lollard Doctrines, Attributed to Wicliffe: Now First Printed from a Manuscript in the Library of Trinity College, Dublin, with an Introduction and Notes by James Henthorn Todd (London: Printed for the Camden Society by John Bowyer Nichols and Son, 1842), Camden Society Publications 20, lxiii, 206, [1] pp.
bibliographic.title: An Apology for Lollard Doctrines, Attributed to Wicliffe
bibliographic.publisher: Printed for the Camden Society by John Bowyer Nichols and Son

notes: Todd's introduction occupies pp. v–lxiii; the Middle English text pp. 1–113; notes pp. 115–188; glossary pp. 189–206; and an unnumbered errata leaf follows.
Todd calls the work the twenty-sixth article under his older 29-item description of MS 245. Jefferson's corrected modern description groups the same work as item 24 in a 27-item contents list.
The title and running title ‘Wicliffe's Apology’ were supplied by Todd and have no manuscript authority.

entities.label: Trinity College Dublin MS 245
entities.description: A first-half-fifteenth-century parchment Lollard miscellany written by one scribe. The Apology is one component at corrected fols. 163r–217r; older descriptions and the current catalog use old fols. 164–218 because the ink foliation skipped 133.

date_statements.label: Jefferson palaeographic date
date_statements.value: first half of the fifteenth century (s. xv¹)

date_statements.label: current TCD catalog date
date_statements.notes: The institutional item record gives a broader century range than Jefferson's scholarly description.

holding.notes: The exact current catalog record is local record CALM00230. Its old-foliation description identifies the Apology at fols. 164–218 by incipit and states ‘Not by Wycliffe.’

physical_description.support: Parchment (described as membrane by Jefferson and vellum by the TCD catalog)
physical_description.extent: Jefferson: iii + 218 + iii leaves; the older/current ink foliation appears to number 219 because it skips 133
physical_description.format: 159 × 108 mm; writing space 119 × 77 mm; one column of 32 lines
notes: Written in a single Anglicana hand with slight Secretary influence.
Blue initials with red decoration, red paraphs and touching, occasional red-underlined biblical quotations, and marginal corrections occur throughout the codex.
The post-medieval binding was rebacked in 1947.
Jefferson records likely sixteenth-century ownership by Thomas Chamber and transfer to the University of Dublin by Charles II.
The corrected work locus is fols. 163r–217r. Todd's fol. 164a–218a and TCD's fols. 164–218 are legacy-foliation equivalents.

Jefferson's analysis places the scribe in the southern Central Midlands and most likely Huntingdonshire, with occasional more northerly forms possibly belonging to the exemplar or scribe. This does not establish the place of composition.
TCD MS 244 is separately digitized and must not be substituted merely because its shelfmark is adjacent.

entities.label: TCD catalog record CALM00230 for IE TCD MS 245
entities.description: The exact current institutional catalog record. It supplies the current shelfmark, broad date, material and extent, physical-access contact, and an old-foliation contents list whose Apology entry says ‘Not by Wycliffe.’
notes: The enclosing title's Wycliffe label is not item-level authorship evidence for every text in the miscellany.

entities.label: Jefferson 1995 scholarly description of TCD MS 245
entities.description: A detailed codicological, contents, and linguistic description embedded in Jefferson's doctoral edition of the Ten Commandments commentary. It is scholarly description of MS 245, not an institutional item record and not a new edition of the Apology.

bibliographic.citation: Judith Anne Jefferson, ‘An Edition of the Ten Commandments Commentary in BL Harley 2398 and the Related Version in Trinity College Dublin 245, York Minster XVI.L.12 and Harvard English 738: Together with Discussion of Related Commentaries’ (PhD thesis, University of Bristol, 1995).
bibliographic.title: An Edition of the Ten Commandments Commentary in BL Harley 2398 and the Related Version in Trinity College Dublin 245, York Minster XVI.L.12 and Harvard English 738

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of the Princeton Theological Seminary Library copy of Todd 1842
entities.description: An independently scanned complete copy of Todd's 1842 volume, hosted by Internet Archive with PDF, DjVu, OCR, and page-image derivatives. It reproduces the printed edition, not TCD MS 245.

physical_description.format: 400 ppi color scan with PDF, DjVu, OCR, and derivative formats
notes: The source copy was contributed by Princeton Theological Seminary Library and scanned at Internet Archive's New Jersey center.

entities.reproduction_format: digital page images of a printed edition

agents.name: The Library of Trinity College Dublin

scope.notes: The identical U-M identifier, header, text, page-image references, and raw-package comparison establish derivation; the exact transfer date and transformation script are undocumented.

subject.description: Electronic title page, Middle English work, page milestones, and linked images

object.description: Todd's title page and Middle English text, with page milestones for omitted editorial matter
locators: page milestones for pp. v–lxiii and 114–206 plus errata

scope.notes: U-M did not transcribe Todd's introduction, long notes, glossary, or errata as continuous text, although their page images remain linked.

object.description: One complete copy of Todd's 1842 volume

scope.notes: These are printed-book images, not images of MS 245.

object.description: The sole manuscript item identified by incipit

scope.notes: Jefferson's corrected locus and Todd/TCD's old locus are equivalent because old foliation skipped 133. Todd printed from this single manuscript and reserved conjectural emendations for his notes.

object.description: TCD MS 245, including the Apology component

editorial_practices.description: Todd selected and printed one untitled item from the larger MS 245 miscellany and supplied the modern title An Apology for Lollard Doctrines.
editorial_practices.scope: Todd pp. 1–113 from corrected fols. 163r–217r (old fols. 164–218)

editorial_practices.representation: Expanded silently in ordinary type; no attempt to reproduce manuscript contractions
editorial_practices.description: Todd explicitly says that he did not represent manuscript contractions. He retained yogh and thorn because he considered them orthographically significant.
notes: Todd's printed spelling therefore cannot by itself show what a particular manuscript abbreviation looked like.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Inconsistent manuscript capitalization of proper names and the name of God
editorial_practices.description: Todd identifies capitalization of proper names and the name of God as one of his permitted deviations from the manuscript.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Todd describes manuscript punctuation as capricious and irregular
editorial_practices.representation: Punctuation conformed to sense and modern notation
editorial_practices.description: Todd did not preserve manuscript punctuation and instead punctuated according to sense with modern notation.

Todd says he retained obvious scribal errors except original-scribe corrections and reserved conjectural emendations for notes; Richard Gibbings assisted in collating proof sheets against the original manuscript.
editorial_practices.scope: Middle English text and Todd's notes

editorial_practices.description: The OCR text transcribes Todd's title page and Middle English work on pp. 1–113 but leaves most of the introduction and back matter as linked page milestones without continuous transcription.
notes: The 394 NOTE1 elements within the encoded body are predominantly short references/summaries from the printed text, not Todd's complete pp. 115–188 notes section.

access.access_method: Read the checked-out CME submodule path
U-M item page and full-text views
access.format: HTML OCR text with linked TIFF page images

access.access_method: Direct Raw Michigan Book ZIP download
access.format: 19,005,255-byte ZIP containing 272 TIFFs and five support files

access.access_method: Internet Archive viewer or direct PDF, DjVu, OCR, and derivative downloads

local_copies.label: Complete Internet Archive PDF of Todd's 1842 edition

access.format: HTML catalog record; JSON API representation

access.access_method: Independent researchers are eligible for a visiting-reader card; book a manuscripts appointment at least one week in advance through TCD's form
access.format: Original parchment manuscript in the Research Collections Study Centre
notes: The catalog currently marks the manuscript available, but appointment confirmation and conservation review govern actual consultation.

access.access_method: No exact public MS 245 image object was located in TCD's current digitized-manuscript list, Digital Collections search, web search, or exact catalog record
notes: The official project list includes adjacent MS 244 and says more material may be discoverable in Digital Collections; absence from the list does not prove MS 245 has never been digitized.

access.access_method: Complete TCD's PDF Image Request Form and email it to Digital.Collections@tcd.ie; all orders require departmental and conservation approval

local_copies.label: Complete TCD Digital Collections Image Request Form
Complete TCD Digital Collections 2023 price list

access.format: High-resolution TIFF or JPEG, or low-resolution JPEG; web-based delivery
notes: The 2023 academic/editorial/research price list gives €25 for setup/first image and €2 per additional image, exclusive of 23% Irish VAT, with at least three weeks' lead time; this is not a quote for MS 245.

Corrected fols. 163r–217r comprise 109 manuscript sides if every side from the first recto through the last recto is requested; TCD must confirm chargeable image count, conservation feasibility, VAT, and any reuse licence.
The form does not make institutional affiliation mandatory in the stated instructions, and TCD's visit page expressly welcomes independent researchers.

access.access_method: University of Bristol thesis landing page or direct PDF

rights.component: The local XML serialization and repository-specific transformations
rights.basis: No item-specific license for the repository serialization was located; public-domain status of the medieval text and 1842 edition does not itself license every modern serialization or repository contribution.

rights.component: Materials presented in U-M CME item ACM9160.0001.001
rights.basis: The current exact U-M item page states that the materials are in the public domain and applies the No Copyright–United States rights statement.
notes: NoC-US is jurisdiction-specific and is not a worldwide public-domain dedication.

rights.component: The electronic text and page images as described in the 2006 embedded AVAILABILITY statement
rights.basis: The XML header says ‘Where applicable, subject to copyright’ and warns that other distribution restrictions may apply. This historical generic statement is preserved separately from U-M's current NoC-US item label.

rights.component: The 272 linked page images as materials of U-M item ACM9160.0001.001
rights.basis: U-M's current exact item page applies No Copyright–United States to the item's materials, including linked page images; the Internet Archive raw-package metadata itself supplies no separate explicit license.
notes: Confirm with U-M before treating the Internet Archive raw ZIP, rather than the U-M-hosted image presentation, as carrying the same provider statement.

rights.component: The anonymous medieval text and Todd's 1842 edition, introduction, notes, glossary, and typographical presentation
rights.basis: The edition was published in London in 1842. Internet Archive's item-specific evidence records no visible copyright notice and possible status NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT for the United States.

rights.component: Internet Archive's modern digital images, OCR, metadata, and derivatives as distinct from the 1842 printed content
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: The Internet Archive metadata's NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT evidence addresses the 1842 source; no separate affirmative license for every modern scan derivative was located.

rights.component: The anonymous medieval text embodied in corrected fols. 163r–217r
rights.basis: The text survives in a manuscript copied in the first half of the fifteenth century and no modern author is asserted; this finding does not cover modern photographs, catalog metadata, or provider contracts.

rights.component: Any new photographs or digital images supplied by TCD for MS 245
rights.jurisdiction: TCD provider policy and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: TCD's general open-licensing policy says eligible digitized out-of-copyright material may be reused under CC BY 4.0, subject to exceptions and other agreements. Its image-order form and 2023 price list separately contemplate publication licensing and require approval. No exact MS 245 image object or item-specific licence was available to resolve which terms would govern newly supplied images.
notes: Ask TCD to state in writing whether ordered MS 245 images receive CC BY 4.0, another licence, or case-specific restrictions before redistributing them or using them in commercial print-on-demand workflows.
Research use of the images and republication of the images are separate questions.

rights.component: Jefferson's thesis PDF and scholarly text
rights.basis: The PDF's University of Bristol cover sheet expressly applies Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-NoDerivatives 4.0 International.
notes: The thesis may be downloaded and cited, but its noncommercial/no-derivatives licence must not be confused with rights in the fifteenth-century manuscript text.

evidence.summary: Establishes the local artifact identity, U-M identifier and 2006 production declaration, Todd source edition, embedded availability statement, uncorrected OCR/TEI Level 1 method, linked TIFF sequence, and encoded text structure.

evidence.citation: Structural, XPath, hash, and Git inspection of General Corpus acm9160.xml.
evidence.locator: git blob aa6c774bdf77509d98f8fb38c1ecf954e34fd53a; submodule commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c
evidence.summary: Counts 30 body DIV1 elements (one prologue and 29 chapters), 394 body NOTE1 elements, and 272 PB milestones (64 front, 113 body, 95 back); records chapter N sequence 1–26, 28, 19, 30 and confirms that only title page plus pp. 1–113 are transcribed as continuous text.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item ACM9160.0001.001.
evidence.summary: Provides the current public item, bibliographic source, supposed-author label, Lollards subject, educational/research access language, and public-domain/No Copyright–United States statement.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for raw Michigan book item acm9160.0001.001.umich.edu.
evidence.summary: Identifies the noindex raw Michigan mirror, ARK, 2011 ingest date, and 19,005,255-byte original Raw Michigan Book ZIP with SHA-1 b20a86f94232756a04e71aefc497ac8af4a57648.

evidence.citation: U-M/Internet Archive Raw Michigan Book ZIP for ACM9160.0001.001.
evidence.summary: Verified public download containing 272 numbered TIFFs plus U-M XML, plain text, header, pageview data, and logs. The ZIP is 19,005,255 bytes (SHA-256 b632e4...), and its 294,377-byte XML has SHA-256 b6b2ea04... and differs from the repository serialization.

evidence.citation: An Apology for Lollard Doctrines, Attributed to Wicliffe, ed. James Henthorn Todd (London: Camden Society, 1842), Internet Archive item apologyforlollar00wycl.
evidence.locator: title page; Introduction pp. v–xxiii and lvi–lxiii; text pp. 1–113; Notes pp. 115–188; Glossary pp. 189–206; errata leaf
evidence.summary: Direct inspection confirms title, date, single witness and old locus, uncertain attribution, supplied title, complete contents, and editorial practices: retained scribal errors, conjectures in notes, expanded contractions, retained thorn/yogh, normalized capitals and punctuation, and partly added marginal summaries/references.

Records Princeton Theological Seminary Library as contributor; 2008-07-17 scan date; 400 ppi and 282 images; OCLC 3317538, LCCN a17001203, Open Library OL23278389M; a 14,143,936-byte PDF; and item-specific US evidence ‘possible-copyright-status: NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT’ based on 1842 date and no visible notice.

evidence.citation: Library of Trinity College Dublin, catalog record CALM00230, IE TCD MS 245, ‘John Wycliffe, tracts.’
evidence.locator: Manuscript tab, CALM00230, call number IE TCD MS 245, description of fols. 164–218
evidence.summary: Exact item-level institutional record: fifteenth-century vellum manuscript, 219 folios, available through Manuscripts and Archives. The old-foliation contents entry identifies the Apology incipit at fols. 164–218 and says ‘Not by Wycliffe.’
notes: The direct API request requires iii-customer-domain: tcd.eu.iiivega.com; the user-facing catalog card is the primary access URL.

evidence.citation: University of Bristol Research Portal record for Judith Anne Jefferson's 1995 doctoral thesis.
evidence.summary: Official repository record confirms author, full title, doctoral-thesis status, 1995 award date, and public PDF availability.

evidence.citation: Judith Anne Jefferson, ‘An Edition of the Ten Commandments Commentary in BL Harley 2398 and the Related Version in Trinity College Dublin 245, York Minster XVI.L.12 and Harvard English 738’ (PhD thesis, University of Bristol, 1995).
evidence.locator: pp. xx–xxvii and lx–lxvii (PDF pp. 21–28 and 61–68); PDF cover rights statement
evidence.summary: Detailed scholarly description dates MS 245 (C.5.6) to s. xv¹, explains the skipped 133 and corrected foliation, describes one Anglicana hand, decoration and provenance, locates the Apology at corrected fols. 163r–217r, and localizes the scribal dialect most probably to Huntingdonshire. The PDF cover applies CC BY-NC-ND 4.0.

evidence.citation: Library of Trinity College Dublin, Manuscripts for Medieval Studies: Digitised Manuscripts.
evidence.summary: The current project list links MS 244 but not MS 245 and notes that more digitized material may exist in Digital Collections. It supports a dated negative public-access finding, not a categorical claim that MS 245 has never been digitized.

evidence.citation: Exact-shelfmark searches of TCD Digital Collections and public web indexes for IE TCD MS 245/C.5.6.
evidence.summary: No exact public digital object or complete MS 245 image sequence was found. Automated access to Digital Collections was CAPTCHA-blocked, so the negative result remains incomplete and date-bounded.
notes: Do not infer from adjacent MS 244 results that MS 245 is digitized.

evidence.citation: Library of Trinity College Dublin, Planning Your Visit / Using Research Collections.
evidence.summary: States that academics, students, and independent researchers are welcome; manuscripts require an appointment at least one week in advance; a visiting-reader card is required; and rescoll@tcd.ie is the contact when an item cannot be located or clarification is needed.

evidence.citation: Library of Trinity College Dublin, Imaging Services.
evidence.summary: Says researchers may request digitization or digital images subject to restrictions; orders use the PDF form emailed to Digital.Collections@tcd.ie; new photography needs at least three weeks; and TCD may refuse an order.

evidence.citation: Library of Trinity College Dublin, Digital Collections Pricelist (2023).
evidence.summary: Lists academic/editorial/research high-resolution TIFF or JPEG imaging at €25 setup/first image and €2 per additional image, exclusive of 23% VAT; gives three-week minimum; and lists separate publication licensing tiers of €45, €90, or €150.

evidence.citation: Library of Trinity College Dublin, Digital Collections Image Request Form.
evidence.summary: Requests item title, folios, TCD shelfmark, desired TIFF/JPEG output and intended publication use; says every order requires departmental and conservation evaluation and may be declined.

evidence.citation: Library of Trinity College Dublin, Rights Information.
evidence.summary: States a general policy that digitized out-of-copyright originals not subject to other agreements may be reused under CC BY 4.0, while exceptions require written approval; directs users to Digital.Collections@tcd.ie to clarify the exact licence or permission.

open_questions.question: Has TCD digitized MS 245, or can it provide an existing complete or fols. 163r–217r image set that is not publicly indexed?
next_steps: Ask rescoll@tcd.ie or Digital.Collections@tcd.ie whether an existing surrogate is available before commissioning new photography.
Request the corrected locus fols. 163r–217r and mention the old-foliation equivalent 164–218 to avoid ambiguity.

open_questions.question: What exact licence and publication conditions would govern newly supplied MS 245 images, including redistribution in a public Git repository and reuse connected with commercial or noncommercial print-on-demand research?
next_steps: Obtain written item-specific terms from Digital.Collections@tcd.ie before redistributing any supplied image files.
Ask whether the general CC BY 4.0 digitized-content policy applies to newly commissioned MS 245 images and whether the older publication-fee schedule is still applicable.
notes: Underlying medieval text rights do not answer modern-image reuse or provider-contract questions.

open_questions.question: What exact process and date produced the repository XML from U-M's electronic item, and which archived U-M serialization was its immediate file-level source?
next_steps: Inspect CME repository history or project documentation for the NODE insertion and EDITORIALDECL numbering transformation.
Retain copied_from at supported/high confidence rather than claiming byte-identical copying.

open_questions.question: How do Todd's expansions, supplied marginal summaries, punctuation, capitalization, and chapter-number anomaly correspond to the manuscript's actual letterforms, abbreviations, colors, and layout?
next_steps: Compare Todd pp. 1–113 with a complete TCD reproduction of corrected fols. 163r–217r.
Record any requested images' exact licence separately before using them as public reference files.

open_questions.question: How should U-M's current No Copyright–United States statement be reconciled with the generic 2006 XML warning that copyright or other distribution restrictions may apply, especially for the raw TIFF ZIP mirror?
next_steps: Ask U-M whether the current item-level NoC-US statement covers downloadable OCR/XML and the raw TIFF package as well as the web presentation.
Preserve both dated provider statements until clarified.
